records.ety: [L. humanitas: cf. F. humanit. See Human.]

defs: The quality of being human; the peculiar nature of man, by which he is distinguished from other beings.

quotes: But hearing oftentimes The still, and music humanity.
It is a debt we owe to humanity.

defs: The quality of being humane; the kind feelings, dispositions, and sympathies of man; especially, a disposition to relieve persons or animals in distress, and to treat all creatures with kindness and tenderness.
Mental cultivation; liberal education; instruction in classical and polite literature.
quotes: Polished with humanity and the study of witty science.

defs: The branches of polite or elegant learning; as language, rhetoric, poetry, and the ancient classics; belles-letters.
notes: ☞ The cultivation of the languages, literature, history, and archæology of Greece and Rome, were very commonly called literæ humaniores, or, in English, the humanities, . . . by way of opposition to the literæ divinæ, or divinity.
